hard.next_step: Discovery Meeting 10/2 Awaiting RFP to be released Check In
hard.ais_why: Feb 2026 Avoma call captures Metroselskabet's Jens inquiring about AI-driven data analysis and automated report drafting, and acknowledging rapid AI adoption in procurement (in-house chatbots already deployed); buyer AI awareness without AI-led selection, and a stated preference for off-the-shelf configurable products over agentic specialty modules.

north_star_verdict.headline: Greenfield Danish public-transit deal stalled ~6.5 months in Qualified; buyer confirmed on 16 Jun that market dialogues are done and a tender is being scoped, but no RFP date and no lean to Zycus among eight vendors.
north_star_verdict.math: Created 2025-12-23, Qualified same day; ~195 days in Qualified as of today 2026-07-06, far beyond the typical 30-60 day qualification window. Last buyer call was the market-dialogue demo 2026-02-23 (~133 days ago). The most recent GENUINE two-way buyer touch is Jens's 2026-06-16 email reply (20 days ago) stating the market dialogues are complete and the tender approach is being decided, with no committed date. Close date 2026-12-31 is 178 days out and remains plausible IF the RFP releases and the tender compresses, but the buyer owns the clock and there is no confirmed timeline, so the date is defensive without a restart. Forecast Pipeline is appropriate for stage and momentum.
evidence: Jens Dlugosch Sonne, 2026-06-16 email: 'we are done with the market dialogues and are currently evaluating how we should approach the tender... you'll hear from us when we're ready' — buyer controls the clock, no committed date
Casper offered a Source-to-Pay RFP template on 2026-06-17 (email); awaiting buyer take-up
Last buyer call was the market-dialogue demo 2026-02-23; the full committee (Ulrik, Peter, Nils, Lars) attended but none has re-engaged since
Stage Qualified, ~195 days in stage (created 2025-12-23), well beyond the 30-60 day norm

One of eight vendors in a formal market dialogue with no down-select; Jens: 'any of the solutions could be very helpful, which makes it very difficult to find out which one is more suiting' (2026-02-20)
LastActivityDate 2026-06-17 reflects Casper's outreach, not a fresh buyer deliverable

metrics.narrative: Metroselskabet is a greenfield: Jens Dlugosch Sonne stated 'we are starting on a very low level, so we don't have any IT solution to assist us with our tasks' (Avoma, 2026-02-20), so any modern suite delivers lift, but the buyer has not quantified the baseline cost of manual work (FTE burn, sourcing cycle time, contract-compliance leakage, missed savings) or a target ROI. The strategic driver is stated qualitatively: 'AI is very important... we need to follow suit with the rest of the market... otherwise we'll just be left behind' (Jens, 2026-02-20). No financial metric has been captured on any call or field. Quantifying the value case with Ulrik Wendelboe and Jens before the RFP is a ranked move.
sources: Avoma alignment call 2026-02-20, Jens Dlugosch Sonne: 'we are starting on a very low level, so we don't have any IT solution to assist us with our tasks'
Avoma alignment call 2026-02-20, Jens: 'AI is very important... otherwise we'll just be left behind'
SF Next_Step: 'Awaiting RFP to be released' — no quantified business case logged

economic_buyer.narrative: Ulrik Wendelboe (Head of Procurement & Contract Management, Salesforce PRIMARY contact, Role = Decision Maker) is the budget owner for this procurement-software initiative and the economic buyer by inference: as the department head he controls the tender and the commercial decision, and Jens reports to him (Casper on 2026-02-20: 'your boss, Ulrich'). Ulrik attended the Feb market-dialogue demo but has had no direct commercial or pricing conversation with Zycus and has been passive since. No pricing, budget-authority, or approval-chain discussion has occurred on any call. The move is to arm Jens with a quantified value model and secure a direct Ulrik conversation to convert passive attendance into active sponsorship before the RFP.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Ulrik Wendelboe, Head of Procurement & Contract Management, Role = Decision Maker, PRIMARY
Avoma alignment call 2026-02-20, Casper: 'your boss, Ulrich' — Jens reports to Ulrik

decision_criteria.narrative: The buyer has articulated clear qualitative criteria: strictly off-the-shelf, well-tested, configurable-within-framework products with NO custom development (Jens, 2026-02-20: 'we are only interested in off the shelf products... not something we will have to develop from scratch... that sounds like a public project and we don't want that'); a suite/unified platform over best-of-breed point solutions; strong native AI (data analysis, AI-drafted reports); and relevant customer references in their sector. These are confirmed from the alignment call. What is not yet known is the formal weighted evaluation criteria and scoring model, which will be set in the forthcoming RFP — hence the move to co-shape those criteria with Jens.
sources: Avoma alignment call 2026-02-20, Jens: 'we are only interested in off the shelf products... not something we will have to develop from scratch'
Avoma alignment call 2026-02-20, Jens asked for AI data analysis and report drafting, and customer references

decision_process.narrative: The buyer is running a formal, structured public-sector-style sourcing: an 8-vendor market dialogue (Jens: 'the 8 different companies that we've been talking to') followed by internal assessment and then a tender/RFP. Jens confirmed on 2026-06-16 that the market-dialogue phase is complete and the team is now deciding how to approach the tender. The buying committee is cross-functional and role-clear: Ulrik (Decision Maker / department head), Jens and Peter (procurement evaluation), and Nils/Niels plus Lars (IT — Niels takes over as project manager post-award, Lars is the systems architect for technical vetting). The gap is the timeline and the approval chain past Ulrik, which the RFP will formalize; securing the tender schedule in writing is a ranked move.
sources: Avoma alignment call 2026-02-20, Jens on the 8-vendor market dialogue and committee roles
Email 2026-06-16, Jens: market dialogues complete, evaluating how to approach the tender

paper_process.narrative: Contracting mechanics are not yet visible: no MSA/SOW, legal, InfoSec, procurement-channel, or PO discussion has occurred because the deal is pre-RFP in Qualified. As a Danish state-owned entity (Metroselskabet), the eventual purchase will run through a formal public-tender and likely EU procurement rules, which will govern paper and timeline. There is no current contract to displace (greenfield). Visibility will come only once the RFP is released and the tender documents define the contracting and legal path; obtaining that timeline is the near-term action.
sources: SF Stage = Qualified, no contracting fields populated
Email 2026-06-16, Jens: tender approach still being scoped

identify_pain.narrative: The operational pain is explicit and owned by procurement: Metroselskabet runs supplier-relationship, contract, and sourcing work with NO supporting IT system ('we are starting on a very low level, so we don't have any IT solution to assist us with our tasks', Jens, 2026-02-20), which is manual and unscalable. A second, strategic pain is competitive AI pressure: Jens (2026-02-20) said they only realized in the last ~1.5 years how central AI is and 'we need to follow suit with the rest of the market... otherwise we'll just be left behind', noting they already deployed in-house chatbots and language models. Pain owner is Jens/Ulrik in procurement. The pain is real but unquantified, which caps urgency.
sources: Avoma alignment call 2026-02-20, Jens: 'we don't have any IT solution to assist us with our tasks'
Avoma alignment call 2026-02-20, Jens: AI adoption over the last 1.5 years, in-house chatbots, 'follow suit or be left behind'

champion.narrative: Jens Dlugosch Sonne (Senior Procurement Manager, SF Role = Evaluator) is a developing champion: he is the accessible process lead, coordinated the Feb demo and the attendee list, articulated the requirements, is conceptually aligned with Zycus's config-first / AI-native / suite positioning ('that's exactly what we're looking for', 2026-02-20), and stays in two-way contact (email 2026-06-16/17). What has developed: consistent access and positive alignment. What is still missing: he has not brokered a one-on-one with Ulrik, has not shared internal evaluation criteria or competitive intel, and has not pushed to accelerate the tender ('you'll hear from us when we're ready') — so he is a friendly guide, not yet an internal advocate driving urgency. Building him into an active champion ranks ahead of any commercial step.
sources: SF OpportunityContactRole: Jens Dlugosch Sonne, Senior Procurement Manager, Role = Evaluator; also the sole task/activity contact
Avoma alignment call 2026-02-20, Jens led the alignment and articulated criteria
Email 2026-06-16 / 2026-06-17, ongoing two-way contact with Casper

competition.narrative: This is an explicit 8-vendor market dialogue named by Jens (2026-02-20). He grouped Zycus with iValue (Ivalua), Jaggaer ('Jaguar'), and Coupa as 'general level' broad-suite vendors, and named Kodiak Hub and Serial Labs as more specialized point players. No vendor has been down-selected or preferred: Jens said 'any of the solutions could be very helpful, which makes it very difficult to find out which one is more suiting'. Zycus's wedge is 100% native AI (no third-party LLM), 50% R&D engineering density, Gartner Leader status (2026), and the buyer's own stated suite-over-point and off-the-shelf preferences — all of which favor Zycus if it can convert Metro's AI urgency into a decision criterion. The threat is parity and inertia, not a named front-runner.
sources: Avoma alignment call 2026-02-20, Jens: 'you, among with iValue and Jaguar and one more account from Coupa... more focused on the general level... Kodiak Hub... Serial Labs'
Avoma alignment call 2026-02-20, Jens: 'any of the solutions right now could actually be very, very helpful'

deal_movement.summary: The last real buyer touch is Jens's 2026-06-16 email confirming the market-dialogue phase is complete and the tender approach is being scoped; Casper followed on 2026-06-17 offering a Source-to-Pay RFP template. Before that the deal had been quiet since the 2026-02-23 market-dialogue demo. Field history shows only null Next_Step edits and no stage/amount/close moves. The pulse is a live-but-slow process on the buyer's clock.

competitive_position.summary: A formal 8-vendor market dialogue in two tiers: broad-suite peers (Zycus, Jaggaer, Coupa, iValue/Ivalua) and specialized point players (Kodiak Hub, Serial Labs). Zycus sits in the buyer-preferred suite tier and aligns to Metro's off-the-shelf, config-first mandate (Jens, 2026-02-20). No vendor is preferred or down-selected: the buyer explicitly finds all suite options viable ('any of the solutions could be very helpful', 2026-02-20), so the field is at parity and the real threat is inertia, not a front-runner. Jaggaer is the strongest current peer threat as the European procurement-suite vendor with Nordics presence and similar config/AI positioning; Coupa and Ivalua are medium suite peers; Kodiak Hub and Serial Labs are low-threat point players relevant only if scope narrows to contract management. Zycus wins by shaping the RFP criteria around native AI, suite value, and off-the-shelf config before the field differentiates.

best_practice_check.summary: Pre-RFP window is the leverage point: shape the criteria, weaponize AI, and re-activate the committee before the tender freezes the field.
flags: Shape the RFP before it is written: co-author the S2P criteria with Jens so native-AI, suite, and off-the-shelf config become scored requirements the other seven vendors can't all match.
Turn AI curiosity into the forcing function: put Merlin data-analysis and AI report-drafting in front of Metro on their own 'follow suit or be left behind' driver, differentiating on 100% native AI.

Multi-thread the committee: Ulrik (DM/budget), Peter (PM), and Niels + Lars (IT) all attended the Feb demo but none has engaged since; re-activate them before the tender rather than relying on Jens alone.

champion_strength.summary: Jens Dlugosch Sonne (Senior Procurement Manager, SF Evaluator) is the developing champion: accessible, aligned with Zycus's config-first / AI-native / suite positioning ('that's exactly what we're looking for', 2026-02-20), the process lead who coordinated the Feb demo, and still in two-way contact (email 2026-06-16/17). He has NOT yet advocated: no one-on-one with Ulrik, no internal criteria or competitive intel shared, and no push to accelerate the tender. He is a friendly guide, not an internal mobilizer. The path forward: arm him with a quantified value case and an AI proof point he can carry to Ulrik, and co-shape the RFP so his voice differentiates Zycus.

ai_positioning_strength.summary: Metro explicitly raised AI as decisive ('follow suit with the rest of the market or be left behind') and asked about AI data analysis and auto-drafted reports, but Zycus has not put a concrete agentic/Merlin proof point in front of them since the Feb demo. The AI story is a live differentiator that is currently under-leveraged.

ai_fit_signal.summary: AIS 6.0 Moderate: Jens showed genuine AI curiosity (data analysis, report drafting, in-house chatbots already deployed) but the buyer prefers off-the-shelf configurable products over agentic specialty modules, so AI is a buying criterion, not the selection driver. Calls corroborate the field: curious, not AI-led.

items.detail: RFP on an open-ended hold with no committed date; buyer owns the clock ('you'll hear from us when we're ready'), making the 2026-12-31 close defensive.
Economic buyer Ulrik Wendelboe has had no direct commercial conversation and has been passive since the Feb demo; alignment rests on a developing champion.
No budget or ROI confirmed; value case is unquantified against a greenfield with no incumbent spend to redirect.
Undifferentiated in a field of eight suite and point vendors; the buyer finds all viable, so parity is a real vulnerability until Zycus shapes the criteria.

confidence_signals.summary: Momentum is thin: ~195 days in Qualified, a single demo, and a tender on hold. The buyer did reply on 16 Jun, so the relationship is live, but there is no committed next step and no Zycus-specific lean.
